political.s: Political donations lean toward Democratic candidates and causes. Company has not reported lobbying the federal government during recent election cycles, and does not maintain a formal PAC, though employee contributions indicate Democratic support.
political.amt: Limited public data; primarily individual employee donations

charity.s: Strong philanthropic commitment with over $46 million donated since 2012. Company established Aerie Real Foundation in 2022 focusing on women's confidence and inclusivity. Supports mental health, youth empowerment, eating disorders awareness, and voting rights organizations including NAACP LDF.
charity.focus: Youth empowerment, mental health, eating disorders awareness, racial justice, disaster relief

environment.s: Mixed environmental record with some progress on water reduction but concerns about transparency and goals achievement. Company set carbon neutrality goal by 2030 and joined U.S. Cotton Trust Protocol. However, missed sustainability targets (e.g., 100% sustainable cotton by 2023) with unclear progress reporting.

labor.s: Company has Supplier Code of Conduct covering ILO principles and prohibits forced labor, child labor, and Uzbek/Turkmen cotton use. Monitors 300+ factories across 20+ countries but limited transparency on audit scores and factory-specific data. Encourages living wages but doesn't mandate them as standard.

dei.s: Strong DEI commitment with Chief Inclusion & Diversity Officer role established and formal IDEA strategy (Inclusion, Diversity, Equity, Access). Company maintains 14+ employee resource groups and has partnered with HBCUs for recruitment. 44% of board members are diverse in gender and ethnicity.

animals.s: No animal testing; prohibits fur, endangered skins, and mulesed wool. Company permits down feathers and leather with responsible sourcing standards. Aerie partners with Free The Girls for bra recycling program benefiting trafficking survivors.

guns.s: Not applicable; company is apparel and accessories retailer with no firearms operations.

privacy.s: Company maintains privacy policies for website and platforms but limited transparency into data security practices. Collects personal data through websites, apps, and stores with standard disclosure of third-party sharing for retail partners and service providers.

execPay.s: CEO Jay Schottenstein's pay declined 34% from $14.9M (2021) to $9.8M (2022) due to lack of incentive compensation. Heavy portion tied to stock awards (performance-dependent). Jennifer Foyle (President/Creative Director) pay also declined 38% to $5.4M. Compensation scores in bottom 20% for employee base per Comparably.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
